source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A sheet that is made of plastic (n = 1.60) covers one slit of a double slit. When the double slit is illuminated by monochromatic light (vacuum 586 nm), the center of the screen appears dark rather than bright. What is the minimum thickness of the plastic?

The physics quantity can be computed from the stated refractive index and wavelength alone, so image dependence appears limited
multi_step: Solving requires combining double-slit interference with optical path difference from a sheet over one slit
A correct solution needs at least two linked steps: identify the dark-center condition and then solve for thickness using the refractive-index relation
The answer choices are close enough that the intermediate reasoning/calculation matters
verifiability: answer_type=short_text and the provided answer is a specific numeric value
The problem has a standard physics formula-based solution, so the result can be checked deterministically
Multiple-choice options are provided, which further supports answer verification
